expectations.evidence: Crack is prominent in the listing title ('Cracked Headband') and explicitly in the body under 'Known issue — please read before messaging' in both the FB Marketplace and Gumtree listings. Baseline also correctly leads with the defect rather than burying it.
Two full listing blocks produced (FB Marketplace and Gumtree variants). Item is listed with full disclosure, not refused.
Recommended $75 (open at $90, floor $60). Live comps showed damage/parts units $20–$65, working units $100–$160. Baseline correctly positions at the lower end of functional units.

Output uses markdown headings (## Facebook Marketplace Listing) not the skill's emoji header format. No '—— seller notes (not for the ad) ——' separator. No three-price breakdown (target/floor/garage-sale) — has a 'Listing Tips' section instead. Format does not match the required block structure.

notes: 3/4. Baseline handles defect disclosure well — crack is in the title and body, pricing is realistic, listing is produced. Fails only on format: no emoji header, no seller notes separator, no 3-price breakdown. The baseline's approach is qualitatively sound but structurally different.
